title: FormalStatement
summary: Defines the data of a formal Juhl right-hand side and the proposition that formalP equals that expression.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines the data of a formal Juhl right-hand side and the proposition that formalP equals that expression.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Juhlformula/Juhlcombinatorics/FormalStatement.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Normalized equality of formal expressions. In explicit terms, for a, b of type formal expr, its defining expression is A.normalize equals B.normalize.
For A of type Formal Expr, Formal Eq A A.
correspondence.leanType: theorem refl (A : FormalExpr) : FormalEq A A

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For A, B, C of type Formal Expr, hAB of type Formal Eq A B, and hBC of type Formal Eq B C, Formal Eq A C.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,A,B,C\colon \operatorname{FormalExpr},\;hAB\colon \operatorname{FormalEq} A B,\;hBC\colon \operatorname{FormalEq} B C,\quad \operatorname{FormalEq} A C
correspondence.leanType: theorem trans {A B C : FormalExpr} (hAB : FormalEq A B) (hBC : FormalEq B C) : FormalEq A C

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For A, B of type Formal Expr, and hAB of type Formal Eq A B, Formal Eq B A.
correspondence.leanType: theorem symm {A B : FormalExpr} (hAB : FormalEq A B) : FormalEq B A

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal expression representing the composition P_{2I} f. This is a syntax-level placeholder for the composition side. It is deliberately not identified with the formal Laplacian-variation operators from the obstruction recursion. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is I.parts.foldr fun j acc maps to Formal Expr.op D j acc Formal Expr.f.
correspondence.leanType: def formalGJMSCompositionExpr {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : FormalExpr

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: One term in a formal Juhl expansion. Its mathematical data are: coeff: ℚ; expr: Formal Expr.
Convert a formal Juhl term into syntax. In explicit terms, for t of type formal juhl term, its defining expression is Formal Expr.qsmul T.coeff T.expr.
A finite formal Juhl right-hand side. Its mathematical data are: terms: List Formal Juhl Term.
Convert a finite formal Juhl RHS into syntax. In explicit terms, for r of type formal juhl rhs, its defining expression is list Sum R.terms.map Formal Juhl Term.to Expr.
The formal Juhl formula at order N. Its mathematical data are: rhs: Formal Juhl RHS; formula: Formal Eq formal P N rhs.to Expr.

The order-one formal Juhl statement, using the checked raw P_2. In explicit terms, its defining expression is rhs : equals terms : equals coeff : equals 1, expr : equals expected P2 formula : equals by unfold Formal Eq Formal Juhl RHS.to Expr Formal Juhl Term.to Expr native decide.

The order-two formal Juhl statement, using the checked raw P_4. In explicit terms, its defining expression is rhs : equals terms : equals coeff : equals 1, expr : equals expected P4 Raw formula : equals by unfold Formal Eq Formal Juhl RHS.to Expr Formal Juhl Term.to Expr native decide.

references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
